published_content.chapterIntroHtml:
第二章 Python 基本语法

这一章从你写下第一个 print 开始，一路学完变量、数字、字符串、布尔、运算符、输入输出和文件读写。每个小节都先讲知识点，再配 2～3 道随堂题。

  • 核心练习的代码框初始为空；浅灰背景上的红色「提示」与浅灰色示例代码仅作参照，点击输入框后提示会消失，请自行键入代码并运行。
  • 扩展练习的代码窗口留给你自己写，每题给了「期望输出」让你知道目标长什么样。
  • 课件已预装 numpy、pandas、matplotlib，画图后调用 plt.show() 即可。

blocks.sectionTitle: 2.1 第一行程序：print 与注释
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.1 第一行程序：print 与注释

Python 程序由解释器一行一行往下执行。要让程序「说话」，最常用的工具就是 print()——把想输出的内容塞进括号里：

print("hello,world")

这里 print 是函数，括号里的 "hello,world" 是参数，运行后屏幕上看到的 hello,world 就是输出。

程序里以 # 开头的部分叫注释，解释器会跳过它，可以用来给自己或别人留说明：

# 这是一行说明
print("hi")  # 行尾也能写注释

初学最常踩的坑是括号或引号写漏了一个，比如 print("hello"，运行时会冒出 SyntaxError: '(' was never closed。看到这种报错，先回去检查括号有没有配对。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1（基础）：用 print 输出 hello,world。
  • 题 2（扩展）：写一行注释，并用 print 输出一句中文。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：在代码框中键入一行 print，体会输出；可对照背景中的浅灰示例。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.2 变量与赋值

变量就是给一个值起名字。Python 用等号 = 来赋值，左边是名字，右边是值：

a = 2
print(a)   # 输出 2
rate = 0.05
print(rate)  # 输出 0.05

关于变量名，记住两条：

  • Python 区分大小写，number 和 Number 是两个完全不同的变量。
  • 变量名要避开 Python 的保留字（如 if、for、class、True），并且要用字母或下划线开头。

变量可以反复重新赋值，后写的值会覆盖前面的：

x = 10
x = x + 1
print(x)   # 输出 11

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1（基础）：定义利率变量 rate 并打印。
  • 题 2（扩展）：体会大小写敏感，a 和 A 是两个变量。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.3 数字、input 与类型转换

Python 中最常见的数字类型是整数（如 3）和浮点数（如 3.14）。它们支持加减乘除、整除 //、求余 %、幂运算 **，跟数学课的算法基本一样。

这里有个最容易栽跟头的地方：input() 让用户输入内容，但它返回的总是字符串。所以下面这段代码：

x = input("x? ")  # 用户输入 1
y = input("y? ")  # 用户输入 2
print(x + y)       # 屏幕显示 12，而不是 3

原因很简单："1" + "2" 是字符串拼接，结果是 "12"。要让它变成数字相加，得用 int() 转一下：

x = int(input("x? "))
y = int(input("y? "))
print(x + y)       # 输入 1、2，输出 3

另一个常用工具是 round(x, n)，把浮点数四舍五入到 n 位小数：round(3.14159, 2) 得到 3.14。

函数还能嵌套调用，写起来更短，但要保证可读性：

print(int(input("x? ")) + int(input("y? ")))

本节练习 3 题：

  • 题 1：把两个字符串数字转成整数后求和。
  • 题 2：用 round 保留两位小数。
  • 题 3（下一块）：体会函数嵌套，一行写完。

blocks.sectionTitle: 2.4 复数、混合运算、增强赋值与 math
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.4 复数、混合运算、增强赋值与 math

除了整数和浮点数，Python 还原生支持复数，写法是 a + bj，比如：

z = 3 + 4j
print(z.real)   # 实部，输出 3.0
print(z.imag)   # 虚部，输出 4.0

不同数字类型可以一起算，结果会「向更宽的类型靠拢」：整数 + 浮点 = 浮点。比如 123 + 4.0 得到 127.0。

变量自增有简写：i = i + 1 可以写成 i += 1；同理 x **= 3 等价于 x = x ** 3。其它二元运算符（-= *= /= %=）都支持这种写法。

更复杂的数学函数放在 math 模块里，用之前要先 import：

import math
print(math.pi)         # 3.141592...
print(math.pow(2, 3))  # 8.0
print(math.sqrt(16))   # 4.0

本节练习 3 题：

  • 题 1：取复数的实部。
  • 题 2：用 math.pow 配合增强赋值。
  • 题 3（下一块）：int(2.7) 与 **= 自测。

blocks.sectionTitle: 2.5 字符串：转义、f-string、索引与切片
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.5 字符串：转义、f-string、索引与切片

字符串可以用单引号、双引号或三引号包起来：

s1 = '徘徊庭树下'
s2 = "i'm fine"
s3 = '''第一行
第二行'''

想在字符串里塞入一个变量的值，用 f-string（前面加个 f，花括号里写变量名）：

name = "小金"
print(f"hello, {name}")   # hello, 小金

字符串本质是一串字符，每个字符都有编号（索引），从 0 开始数，也可以用负号从右往左数（-1 是最后一个）：

s = "JIT代表金陵科技学院"
print(s[2])      # 第 3 个字符：代
print(s[-1])     # 最后一个字符：院
print(s[0:3])    # 切片：前 3 个，JIT
print(s[::-1])   # 倒着取整个字符串

字符串还能 +（拼接）和 *（重复）：'奥' + '利奥' * 2 得到 '奥利奥利奥'。

本节练习 3 题：

  • 题 1：用 f-string 拼一句话。
  • 题 2：切片实现字符串倒序。
  • 题 3（下一块）：str() 配合 *。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.5 续：数字转字符串、字符串重复

字符串和数字之间不会自动转换。想把数字 3 当成字符使用，先用 str(3) 转一下，得到字符串 '3'，再 * 2 就能得到 '33'。对比：

n = 3
print(n * 2)        # 数字相乘，得到 6
print(str(n) * 2)   # 字符串重复，得到 33
  • 题 3：用 str 和 * 输出 33。
  • 小自测：用 len 数中文字符串长度。

guideCell.codeBackdropCode:

print(str(n) * 2)

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.6 字符串方法与 format

字符串自带很多方法，调用形式是「字符串.方法名()」。常用的几个：

  • strip()：去掉首尾的空白字符。
  • lower() / upper() / title()：大小写转换。
  • find('xxx')：找出 xxx 第一次出现的位置。
  • replace('A', 'B')：把所有 A 替换成 B。
  • split('分隔符')：按分隔符切成一个列表。

方法可以串起来调用，比如清洗用户输入：

name = "   zhang san   "
print(name.strip().title())   # 'Zhang San'

另一个生成格式化文本的工具是 format，模板里用 {} 占位，按顺序填进去：

p = "{0}是{1}的英文缩写"
print(p.format("JIT", "金陵科技学院"))

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：用 format 拼一段营收描述。
  • 题 2：链式调用 strip().replace()。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.7 布尔、比较与逻辑运算

布尔类型只有两个值：True（真）和 False（假）。它们通常由比较运算符产生：

print(3 < 5)      # True
print(3 == 5)     # False
print("a" < "b") # True，按字典序比较

多个条件组合用逻辑运算符：and（都成立才为真）、or（有一个真就为真）、not（取反）：

print((3 < 5) and (1 == 1))   # True
print((3 > 5) or (1 == 1))    # True

有个常见陷阱：0.1 + 0.2 == 0.3 的结果是 False，因为浮点数在二进制里只能近似表示，0.1 + 0.2 实际上是 0.30000000000000004。判断浮点相等时要小心。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：组合比较与逻辑运算。
  • 题 2：感受浮点误差。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.8 输入清洗与文件读写

用户输入往往不干净——首尾带空格、姓名忘了大写。链式调用方法可以一行搞定：

name = input("姓名? ").strip().title()
print(f"hello, {name}")

读写文件用 open()，但更推荐 with open(...) as f: 的写法，它会在缩进块结束时自动关闭文件，省心。三种常用模式：

  • 'w'：写入（覆盖原内容）。
  • 'r'：读取。
  • 'a'：追加（在末尾续写）。

例子：

with open("name.txt", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("张三\n李四")

with open("name.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    print(f.readline().strip())   # 只读第一行：张三

本节练习 3 题：

  • 题 1：链式 strip().title()。
  • 题 2：写入文件再读出第一行。
  • 题 3（下一块）：追加模式 'a'。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
2.8 续：追加模式 'a'

用 'w' 模式打开文件会清空原内容；想在末尾续写，用 'a'：

with open("x.txt", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("A")
with open("x.txt", "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("B")
# 文件最终内容：AB
  • 题 3：先写 A，再追加 B，读出来是 AB。
  • 小自测：用 readlines 读多行。

guideCell.description: 题 3：先 'w' 写入 A，再 'a' 追加 B，读出整个文件并打印。

    f.write("A")
with open("ch2_a.txt", "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write("B")
with open("ch2_a.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    print(f.read())

extensionCell.promptHtml: 小自测：把两行内容写入 ch2_lines.txt，用 readlines() 读回，打印总行数。

with open("ch2_lines.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    print(f"行数={len(f.readlines())}")

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
本章知识点的扩展练习

下面 5 道扩展题把本章学到的语法和金融常用工具（math、pandas、matplotlib、numpy、文件 IO）串起来用。代码窗口留给你自己写，每题给了「期望输出」让你对照。

  • 扩展 1：DayDayUp365——指数的力量。
  • 扩展 2：用 pandas 建一张小行情表。

guideCell.description:
扩展 1：一年 365 天，每天能力值变化 ±1‰。

  • 用 math.pow 算出全年「向上」累计：(1 + 0.001) ** 365。
  • 再算「向下」累计：(1 - 0.001) ** 365。
  • 用 format 输出，保留两位小数，格式：向上：1.44，向下：0.69。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：请参考灰色代码，在下方代码框中手动输入并运行。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
import math
up = math.pow(1 + 0.001, 365)
down = math.pow(1 - 0.001, 365)
print("向上：{:.2f}，向下：{:.2f}".format(up, down))

extensionCell.promptHtml:
扩展 2：用 pandas.DataFrame 建一张小行情表：

  • 两列：code 和 price。
  • 两行：('AAA', 10.0)、('BBB', 20.0)。
  • 直接 print 这张表。
extensionCell.starterCode: # 在此编写你的代码

屏幕大致显示：

0  AAA   10.0
1  BBB   20.0

published_content.chapterIntroHtml:
第三章 Python 的程序控制结构

本章学习 Python 程序控制结构，包括分支、循环、异常处理、函数定义与调用、递归，以及用嵌套循环打印图形。练习重点是让程序根据条件做选择、重复执行任务、处理可能出现的错误，并把重复逻辑封装成函数。

  • 分支结构：用 if、elif、else 和 match 表达选择逻辑。
  • 循环结构：用 for、while、range 遍历序列或重复执行代码。
  • 循环控制：用 continue 跳过本轮，用 break 结束整个循环。
  • 异常处理：用 try...except...else 处理转换、输入等可能失败的情况。
  • 函数与递归：用 def 定义函数，用 return 返回结果，也可以让函数调用自身解决递归问题。

预加载资料说明：部分题目会隐藏预加载 scores、raw_values 或 words。题面会说明变量名、数据类型和每个元素含义。

blocks.sectionTitle: 3.1 if、elif 与 else 分支结构
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.1 if、elif 与 else 分支结构

if 用来判断条件是否成立，elif 用来继续判断其他情况，else 用来处理剩余情况。课件中的空气质量、大小比较和成绩等级都属于分支结构。

score = 85
if score >= 90:
    print('Grade:A')
elif score >= 80:
    print('Grade:B')
else:
    print('Grade:C or below')

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：根据 PM2.5 数值判断空气质量。
  • 题 2：根据成绩输出等级。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：给定 pm = 75，如果 0 <= pm < 35 就输出 空气质量好，否则输出 空气质量不好。最后打印格式为 PM=75, 结论=空气质量不好。

passRule.expectedSubstring: PM=75, 结论=空气质量不好

guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：PM=75, 结论=空气质量不好。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：先用 if 判断 pm 是否落在 0 到 35 之间，再把结论打印出来。

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：给定 score = 86，用 if/elif/else 判断成绩等级：90 分及以上为 A，80 到 89 为 B，70 到 79 为 C，60 到 69 为 D，60 以下为 F。打印 score=86, grade=B。
extensionCell.starterCode:
score = 86
# 在下方用 if/elif/else 判断 grade
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：score=86, grade=B。

blocks.sectionTitle: 3.1 续：and 条件与 match 匹配
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.1 续：and 条件与 match 匹配

多个条件可以用 and、or 组合。Python 也支持 match...case，适合把固定取值映射到不同结果。课件中 Harry、Hermione、Ron 对应 Gryffindor，Draco 对应 Slytherin。

name = 'Harry'
match name:
    case 'Harry' | 'Hermione' | 'Ron':
        print('Gryffindor')
    case 'Draco':
        print('Slytherin')
    case _:
        print('who?')

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 3：用 and 判断成绩区间。
  • 题 4：用 match 判断学院。

guideCell.description: 题 3（基础）：给定 score = 72，用 and 判断它是否在 70 到 79 之间。打印 score=72, 是否C档=True。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：用 score >= 70 and score < 80 得到布尔值。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
score = 72
is_c = score >= 70 and score < 80
print(f'score={score}, 是否C档={is_c}')

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 4（扩展）：给定 name = 'Hermione'，用 match 判断学院。Harry、Hermione、Ron 输出 Gryffindor；Draco 输出 Slytherin；其他名字输出 who?。打印 Hermione-Gryffindor。
extensionCell.starterCode:
name = 'Hermione'
# 在下方用 match/case 判断 house
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：Hermione-Gryffindor。
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
name = 'Hermione'
match name:
    case 'Harry' | 'Hermione' | 'Ron':
        house = 'Gryffindor'
    case 'Draco':
        house = 'Slytherin'
    case _:
        house = 'who?'
print(f'{name}-{house}')

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.2 for 循环与 range

for 循环会从可遍历对象中依次取出元素。range(n) 生成从 0 到 n-1 的整数序列；字符串也可以被逐字符遍历。

for i in range(3):
    print(i)

for s in 'python':
    print(s)

预加载资料字段说明：本节扩展题会预加载 words，它是字符串 python，每个字符都可以通过 for 循环取出。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：累计 range 生成的数字。
  • 题 2：遍历字符串并拼接字符。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：用 for i in range(5) 计算 0 到 4 的总和，打印 range总和=10。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：先设置 total = 0，再在 for 循环里累加 i。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
total = 0
for i in range(5):
    total += i
print(f'range总和={total}')

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：题目已预加载 words，它是字符串 python。请遍历字符串，跳过字符 t，把剩下的字符拼成新字符串并打印 去掉t=pyhon。

extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：去掉t=pyhon。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.2 续：while 循环与 break/continue

while 会在条件成立时重复执行代码。continue 会跳过本轮后续代码并进入下一轮，break 会直接结束整个循环。

count = 0
while count < 3:
    print('programming is fun')
    count += 1

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 3：用 while 生成重复文本。
  • 题 4：结合 break 和 continue 过滤数字。

guideCell.description: 题 3（基础）：用 while 循环把字符串 miao 加入列表 3 次，再用 | 拼接并打印 miao|miao|miao。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：while 循环里每次 append 一个 miao，并记得让 count 加 1。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
count = 0
items = []
while count < 3:
    items.append('miao')
    count += 1
print('|'.join(items))

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 4（扩展）：遍历 range(1, 10)，遇到偶数用 continue 跳过；遇到大于 7 的数字用 break 结束循环。把保留下来的数字打印为 保留数字=1,3,5,7。
extensionCell.starterCode:

# 在下方使用 for、continue 和 break
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：保留数字=1,3,5,7。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.3 异常处理 try、except、else

当代码可能发生错误时，可以使用 try...except 捕捉异常。else 中放转换成功后才执行的代码。课件中的 int(input(...)) 报 ValueError 后，可以用异常处理给出友好提示。

预加载资料字段说明：本节扩展题会预加载 raw_values，它是字符串列表，每个字符串代表一次待转换输入。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：捕捉 ValueError。
  • 题 2：批量转换并统计成功数量。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：给定 raw = 'cat'，尝试把它转成整数。转换失败时捕捉 ValueError，打印 x is not an integer。
guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：x is not an integer。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：把 int(raw) 放进 try，ValueError 分支打印错误提示。

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：题目已预加载 raw_values，它是字符串列表，元素依次为 3、cat、4.5、8。请逐个尝试转换为 float，跳过失败项，统计成功数量和总和，打印 成功数量=3, 总和=15.5。
extensionCell.starterCode: # raw_values 已预加载，请用 try/except 完成统计
extensionCell.setupCode:

# raw_values 已预加载；它是字符串列表，每个元素代表一次待转换输入

blocks.sectionTitle: 3.4 函数的定义、调用与返回值
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.4 函数的定义、调用与返回值

函数用 def 定义，可以接收参数、执行代码并用 return 返回结果。课件中的生日祝福函数、奇偶判断函数都体现了函数封装。

def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 0

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：定义问候函数。
  • 题 2：定义奇偶判断函数。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：定义函数 happy(name)，返回 Happy birthday to you,名字!。调用 happy('Alice') 并打印 Happy birthday to you,Alice!。
guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：Happy birthday to you,Alice!。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：函数里用 return 返回字符串，函数外调用并 print。

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：定义函数 is_even(n)，偶数返回 True，奇数返回 False。调用 is_even(8) 和 is_even(7)，打印 8是偶数=True, 7是偶数=False。
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：8是偶数=True, 7是偶数=False。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.5 递归函数

递归是函数在定义中调用自身。递归函数通常包含两个部分：基础情况用于停止递归，递归情况用于把问题变小。课件中的阶乘和字符串反转都可以用递归完成。

def fact(n):
    if n == 0:
        return 1
    return n * fact(n - 1)

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：递归计算阶乘。
  • 题 2：递归反转字符串。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：定义递归函数 fact(n)，当 n == 0 时返回 1，否则返回 n * fact(n-1)。打印 fact(5)=120。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：递归要先写停止条件 n == 0，再写 n * fact(n-1)。

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：定义递归函数 reverse(s)。当 s 是空字符串时返回空字符串；否则返回 reverse(s[1:]) + s[0]。打印 反转=院学技科陵金。
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：反转=院学技科陵金。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
3.6 嵌套循环：列、行和方块

嵌套循环可以同时控制行和列。外层循环负责每一行，内层循环负责每一行里的字符。课件中的 mario.py 方块可以用嵌套循环，也可以把打印一行封装成函数。

for i in range(3):
    for j in range(3):
        print('#', end='')
    print()

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：打印固定宽度的一行。
  • 题 2：打印方块。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：定义函数 print_row(width)，返回由 ? 重复 width 次形成的字符串。调用 print_row(4) 并打印 ????。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：字符串可以用乘法重复，例如 ? * 4。

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：定义函数 print_square(size)，返回 size 行、每行 size 个 # 的字符串。调用 print_square(3)，把换行替换成 | 后打印 方块=###|###|###。

published_content.chapterIntroHtml:
第四章 Python 组合数据类型

本章学习 Python 的组合数据类型，包括序列类型、集合类型和映射类型。重点掌握元组、列表、集合、字典、列表推导式，以及 map、filter、reduce 等函数式编程工具。

  • 序列类型：字符串、元组、列表都支持索引和切片。
  • 列表：有序且可修改，支持增删改查和循环遍历。
  • 集合：元素唯一，支持成员测试、去重和集合关系运算。
  • 字典：通过键查找值，适合表达映射关系和结构化记录。
  • 推导式与函数式编程：用简洁方式完成筛选、映射和累积计算。

预加载资料说明：部分题目会隐藏预加载 universities、schools、students、records 或 numbers。题面会说明变量名、数据类型、字段名和字段含义。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
4.1 组合数据概述与元组

组合数据类型可以同时保存多个数据项。序列类型有顺序，可用索引访问，常见类型包括字符串、元组和列表；集合类型强调唯一元素；映射类型用键查找值，Python 中对应字典。

元组用小括号定义，具有有序、支持重复、创建后保持固定结构等特点，适合表达固定数据项、函数多返回值和多变量同步赋值。

example_tuple = ('北京大学', '中国人民大学', '清华大学')
print(example_tuple[0])
print(example_tuple[:2])

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：元组索引与切片。
  • 题 2：函数返回元组与拆包。

guideCell.setupCode:
universities = ('北京大学', '中国人民大学', '清华大学')
# universities 已预加载；它是元组，元素顺序固定，可通过索引和切片访问
guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：题目已预加载 universities 元组，包含 北京大学、中国人民大学、清华大学。请打印第 1 个元素、最后 1 个元素和前 2 个元素数量，格式为 首项=北京大学, 末项=清华大学, 前两项数量=2。

passRule.expectedSubstring: 首项=北京大学, 末项=清华大学, 前两项数量=2

guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：首项=北京大学, 末项=清华大学, 前两项数量=2。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：universities 已预加载。它是元组，可用 [0]、[-1]、[:2] 访问。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
first = universities[0]
last = universities[-1]
front = universities[:2]
print(f'首项={first}, 末项={last}, 前两项数量={len(front)}')

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：定义函数 example_func(x)，返回 (x, x*2)。调用 example_func(6) 后拆包为 a、b，打印 拆包结果=6和12。
extensionCell.exerciseTitle: 4.1-题2 函数返回元组与拆包

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
4.2 列表创建、访问与更新

列表是常用的有序序列类型，可以保存不同类型的数据项，也支持嵌套列表。列表可以通过索引、切片访问，并能通过索引赋值或切片赋值更新内容。

example_list = ['中国人民大学', 1949, ['RUC', 100872]]
print(example_list[0])
print(example_list[2][0])

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：访问嵌套列表。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
4.2 列表增删与循环

列表可用 append() 添加元素，用 del、remove()、pop() 删除元素。列表也可通过 for...in... 遍历，或结合 range(len(...)) 输出编号。

students = ['Hermione', 'Harry', 'Ron']
for i in range(len(students)):
    print(i + 1, students[i])

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 3：append 与 remove。
  • 题 4：带编号遍历列表。

guideCell.description: 题 3（基础）：创建列表 ['中国人民大学', 1949, '清华大学']，追加 北京大学，再删除 1949，打印 列表长度=3, 是否含北京大学=True。

schools.remove(1949)

blocks.sectionTitle: 4.3 集合创建、成员测试与关系运算
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
4.3 集合创建、成员测试与关系运算

集合是无序且元素唯一的组合类型，可用 set() 或大括号创建。集合支持成员测试、添加、删除，也支持交集 &、并集 |、差集 - 和对称差集 ^。

example_set = {'北京大学', '中国人民大学', '清华大学'}

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
4.4 字典创建、访问与遍历

字典是映射类型，通过键查找值。可以用 {} 或 dict() 创建字典，通过 [] 或 get() 访问。遍历字典时，for key in dict 会依次得到键。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
4.4 字典列表、修改与删除

多个字典可以放在列表中，用来表达结构相同的记录。字典可以通过赋值修改或新增键值对，也可以用 del 或 pop() 删除键值对。

blocks.sectionTitle: 4.5 列表推导式与函数式编程
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
4.5 列表推导式与函数式编程

列表推导式可以快速生成新列表，也能配合条件筛选。函数式编程中常见 map()、filter() 和 functools.reduce()：map 用于逐项映射，filter 用于筛选，reduce 用于累积。

预加载资料字段说明：本节部分题目会预加载 numbers，它是整数列表，可用于推导式、map、filter 和 reduce 练习。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：列表推导式生成平方。
  • 题 2：map/filter/reduce 综合。

guideCell.setupCode:
numbers = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
# numbers 已预加载；它是整数列表，可用于列表推导式、map、filter、reduce 练习
guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：题目已预加载 numbers，它是整数列表 [0,1,2,3,4,5]。请用列表推导式生成偶数的平方，打印 偶数平方=[0, 4, 16]。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：numbers 已预加载。用 [x*x for x in numbers if x%2==0] 生成偶数平方。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
squares = [x * x for x in numbers if x % 2 == 0]
print(f'偶数平方={squares}')

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：题目已预加载 numbers。请用 map 计算每个数的平方，用 filter 筛选偶数，用 reduce 计算 [1,2,3,4,5] 的乘积。打印 平方末项=25, 偶数=[0, 2, 4], 乘积=120。
extensionCell.starterCode:
from functools import reduce
# numbers 已预加载，请完成 map/filter/reduce
extensionCell.setupCode:
numbers = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
# numbers 已预加载；它是整数列表，可用于列表推导式、map、filter、reduce 练习
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
from functools import reduce
squares = list(map(lambda x: x * x, numbers))
evens = list(filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0, numbers))
product = reduce(lambda x, y: x * y, [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
print(f'平方末项={squares[-1]}, 偶数={evens}, 乘积={product}')

published_content.chapterIntroHtml:
第五章 Python 面向对象编程

本章围绕 Python 面向对象编程展开，学习类、实例、属性、方法、访问控制、继承、多态、鸭子类型、运算符重载、变量引用关系、可变对象和不可变对象。

  • 类与对象：用 class 定义类，用 __init__ 初始化属性，用 self 访问当前对象。
  • 属性访问控制：用双下划线私有属性配合公开方法读取或修改对象状态。
  • 继承与多态：子类继承基类方法，也能重写方法形成不同对象行为。
  • 运算符重载：用 __add__、__sub__、__str__ 等特殊方法定制对象运算和显示。
  • 引用与可变性：理解变量指向对象、列表可原地修改、元组通过重新赋值形成新值。

预加载资料说明：部分题目会隐藏预加载 Animal、Account 或 fruits。题面会说明可直接使用的变量名、类名、属性和方法含义。

blocks.sectionTitle: 5.1 面向对象编程简介与类的基本写法
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
5.1 面向对象编程简介与类的基本写法

面向对象编程把数据和操作数据的方法放在同一个类中。类像模板，实例是根据类创建出来的具体对象。课件用学生成绩举例，把 name 和 score 绑定到对象上，再通过方法打印成绩。

示例说明：类中常见的 __init__ 方法用于初始化对象属性，self 表示当前实例。

class Student:
    def __init__(self, name, score):
        self.name = name
        self.score = score
    def print_score(self):
        print(f'{self.name}: {self.score}')

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：定义 Student 类并打印成绩。
  • 题 2：把过程式成绩数据改成对象方式调用。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：定义 Student 类，在 __init__ 中保存 name 和 score，定义 print_score 方法打印 姓名: 分数。创建 Student('michael', 98) 并调用方法，输出 michael: 98。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：按题意定义类、创建对象并打印指定结果。

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：定义 Student 类，包含 print_score1 方法，输出格式为 kristen的成绩为93。创建 Student('kristen', 93) 并调用该方法。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
5.2 自定义类、属性与方法

自定义类通过 class 定义。属性记录对象状态，方法描述对象能执行的操作。类名通常使用大写字母开头的单词。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：用构造方法绑定属性。
  • 题 2：给类增加计算类方法。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：定义 Book 类，初始化 title 和 price 两个属性。创建 Book('Python', 68)，打印 书名=Python, 价格=68。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
5.3 属性访问控制

属性名前加双下划线可以形成类内部使用的私有属性。外部代码通常通过公开方法读取或修改这些属性，例如 get_name() 和 set_name()。

预加载资料字段说明：本节部分题目会预加载 Account 类。owner 是公有属性，__balance 是私有余额，通过 get_balance() 和 deposit(amount) 访问。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：私有属性读取方法。
  • 题 2：私有属性修改方法。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
5.4 继承：基类和子类

继承可以让子类自动拥有基类的方法，并在需要时增加新方法或重写已有方法。课件示例中 Dog 继承 Animal 后可以调用 run()。

预加载资料字段说明：部分题目会预加载 Animal 基类，它有 run() 方法，返回 Animal is running。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
5.5 多态、鸭子类型与运算符重载

多态让不同子类响应同一个方法名时表现出各自行为。鸭子类型关注对象是否提供所需方法。运算符重载通过特殊方法实现，例如 __add__ 控制 +，__str__ 控制打印格式。

blocks.sectionTitle: 5.6 变量和对象的引用关系
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
5.6 变量和对象的引用关系

Python 中变量保存的是对象引用。多个变量可以指向同一个列表对象；通过其中一个变量修改列表，另一个变量看到的是同一个列表的最新内容。给变量重新赋值会让它指向新的对象。

预加载资料字段说明：部分题目会预加载 fruits，它是可变列表，初始值为 ['apple', 'banana']。

  • 题 1：观察两个变量指向同一列表。
  • 题 2：重新赋值后比较两个变量内容。

fruits = ['apple', 'banana']
# fruits 已预加载；它是 list，可原地修改。

guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：fruits 已预加载。basket = fruits 后，两个变量指向同一个列表对象。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:

basket.append('cherry')
print(f'fruits长度={len(fruits)}, basket最后={basket[-1]}')

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：题目已预加载 fruits。令 basket = fruits，向 basket 追加 cherry，再把 fruits 重新赋值为 ['grape', 'orange']。打印 fruits首项=grape, basket长度=3。

basket.append('cherry')
fruits = ['grape', 'orange']
print(f'fruits首项={fruits[0]}, basket长度={len(basket)}')

published_content.chapterIntroHtml:
第六章 Python 数值计算

本章围绕 numpy 展开，学习数组创建、数组属性、形状改变、索引切片、数组运算、比较操作和科学计算实践。学生端已预装 NumPy，练习中直接使用 import numpy as np。

  • 数组创建：用 np.array()、np.ones()、np.arange() 创建向量和矩阵。
  • 数组结构：理解 ndim、shape、size、dtype、itemsize。
  • 形状与切片：练习 reshape、resize、swapaxes、flatten、索引和切片。
  • 数组运算：练习逐元素算术、比较、统计和条件筛选。
  • 科学计算实践：参考课件中的姓名统计与姓名相似度矩阵案例。

预加载资料说明：部分实践题会隐藏预加载 name 或 scores。题面会说明变量名、数据结构和每个字段/维度含义，学生只需要基于这些变量继续写 NumPy 代码。

blocks.sectionTitle: 6.1 NumPy 库简介与数组创建
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
6.1 NumPy 库简介与数组创建

numpy 是 Python 数值计算常用工具包，核心对象是 ndarray。它适合表达向量、矩阵和多维数组。本章学生端已预装 NumPy，代码中直接写 import numpy as np 即可。

示例说明：用 np.array() 可以把 Python 列表转换为 NumPy 数组；创建时也可以通过 dtype 指定元素类型。

import numpy as np
x = np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]])
print(x)

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：创建二维数组并查看形状和总和。
  • 题 2：创建指定整数类型数组与全 1 数组。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：导入 numpy 并命名为 np，创建数组 [[1,2,3],[4,5,6]]，打印数组形状和元素总和，格式为 数组形状=(2, 3), 总和=21。
guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：数组形状=(2, 3), 总和=21。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：请按题意补全 NumPy 代码，运行后观察终端输出。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
import numpy as np
x = np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]])
print(f"数组形状={x.shape}, 总和={x.sum()}")

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：创建 np.array([4,5,6], dtype=np.int32)，再创建形状为 (3,2) 的全 1 数组。请打印 y 的数据类型和全 1 数组的形状，格式为 dtype=int32, ones形状=(3, 2)。
extensionCell.starterCode:
import numpy as np
# 创建 y 和 a 后打印要求的结果
extensionCell.exerciseTitle: 6.1-题2 dtype 与 ones 创建
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：dtype=int32, ones形状=(3, 2)。
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
import numpy as np
y = np.array([4, 5, 6], dtype=np.int32)
a = np.ones(shape=(3, 2))
print(f"dtype={y.dtype}, ones形状={a.shape}")

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
6.2 ndarray 常用属性与 reshape

ndarray 常用属性包括 ndim、shape、size、dtype 和 itemsize。改变形状时可用 reshape() 返回新数组，也可用 resize() 修改原数组。

示例说明：下面示例用全 1 数组查看属性，再把 3×4 数组重塑为 2×6。

a = np.ones(shape=(4,5))
print(a.ndim, a.shape, a.size, a.dtype, a.itemsize)
b = np.ones((3,4)).reshape(2,6)

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：查看数组属性。
  • 题 2：使用 reshape 和 flatten。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：创建形状为 (4,5) 的全 1 数组，打印维度、形状和元素总数，格式为 维度=2, 形状=(4, 5), 元素数=20。
guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：维度=2, 形状=(4, 5), 元素数=20。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：请按题意补全 NumPy 代码，运行后观察终端输出。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
import numpy as np
a = np.ones(shape=(4, 5))
print(f"维度={a.ndim}, 形状={a.shape}, 元素数={a.size}")

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：用 np.arange(12) 创建 0 到 11 的一维数组，先 reshape 成 (3,4)，再 flatten 成一维数组。请打印 reshape形状=(3, 4), flatten最后=11。
extensionCell.starterCode:
import numpy as np
arr = np.arange(12)
# 在下方完成 reshape 和 flatten
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：reshape形状=(3, 4), flatten最后=11。
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
import numpy as np
arr = np.arange(12)
b = arr.reshape(3, 4)
flat = b.flatten()
print(f"reshape形状={b.shape}, flatten最后={flat[-1]}")

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
6.3 swapaxes、索引与切片

NumPy 数组支持按位置取值、负数下标和切片。二维数组可以用 b[行范围, 列范围] 同时截取行和列。swapaxes(0,1) 可以交换二维数组的行轴和列轴。

示例说明：一维数组可写 a[2:5:2] 取从第 2 位到第 4 位、步长为 2 的元素；二维数组可写 b[2:4,1:3] 截取子矩阵。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：交换二维数组的轴。
  • 题 2：完成一维与二维切片。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：创建数组 [[1,2,3],[4,5,6]]，用 swapaxes(0,1) 交换行和列，打印交换后数组的形状和第一列，格式为 交换后形状=(3, 2), 第一列=[1, 2, 3]。
guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：交换后形状=(3, 2), 第一列=[1, 2, 3]。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：请按题意补全 NumPy 代码，运行后观察终端输出。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]])
b = a.swapaxes(0, 1)
print(f"交换后形状={b.shape}, 第一列={b[:,0].tolist()}")

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：创建 a=np.arange(10) 和 b=np.arange(15).reshape(5,3)。请打印 a[2:7:2]，再打印 b[2:4,1:3] 的元素总和，格式为 一维切片=[2, 4, 6], 二维切片和=36。
extensionCell.starterCode:
import numpy as np
# 创建数组并完成切片

passRule.expectedSubstring: 一维切片=[2, 4, 6], 二维切片和=36

extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：一维切片=[2, 4, 6], 二维切片和=36。
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
import numpy as np
a = np.arange(10)
b = np.arange(15).reshape(5, 3)
part1 = a[2:7:2]
part2 = b[2:4, 1:3]
print(f"一维切片={part1.tolist()}, 二维切片和={part2.sum()}")

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
6.4 NumPy 数组运算与比较

NumPy 支持数组逐元素运算，例如 np.add、np.subtract、np.multiply、np.divide、np.power 和 np.remainder。比较运算会返回布尔数组，例如 np.equal、np.greater、np.less_equal。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：完成数组加法、乘法和幂运算。
  • 题 2：完成数组比较并统计结果。

guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：令 a=np.array([4,5,6])、b=np.array([1,2,3])，分别计算加法、乘法和幂运算，打印 加法=[5, 7, 9], 乘法=[4, 10, 18], 幂末项=216。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：请按题意补全 NumPy 代码，运行后观察终端输出。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([4, 5, 6])
b = np.array([1, 2, 3])
add = np.add(a, b)
mul = np.multiply(a, b)
pow_arr = np.power(a, b)
print(f"加法={add.tolist()}, 乘法={mul.tolist()}, 幂末项={pow_arr[-1]}")

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：令 a=np.array([4,1,2])、b=np.array([2,3,2])。请计算 a>b 的个数，以及 a==b 的个数，打印 大于个数=1, 相等个数=1。
extensionCell.starterCode:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([4, 1, 2])
b = np.array([2, 3, 2])
# 在下方完成比较统计

passRule.expectedSubstring: 大于个数=1, 相等个数=1

extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：大于个数=1, 相等个数=1。
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
import numpy as np
a = np.array([4, 1, 2])
b = np.array([2, 3, 2])
greater_count = np.greater(a, b).sum()
equal_count = np.equal(a, b).sum()
print(f"大于个数={greater_count}, 相等个数={equal_count}")

blocks.sectionTitle: 6.5 NumPy 科学计算实践：姓名统计与相似度
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
6.5 NumPy 科学计算实践：姓名统计与相似度

本节参考课件中的一年级新生姓名案例。已知 11 位小朋友姓名数组，需要先统计不同姓名出现次数，再构造姓名相似度矩阵。这里的相似度规则是：自己和自己为 1；两个不同位置的姓名相同，则相似度为该姓名在除自己外全部同学中的占比；姓名不同则为 0。

预加载资料字段说明：实践题会预加载变量 name，它是一维 NumPy 字符串数组，每个元素是一位小朋友的姓名。可直接使用 np.unique(name, return_inverse=True)、np.add.at、np.vstack(...).T 等方法。

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：统计不同姓名出现次数。
  • 题 2：计算姓名相似度矩阵中的指定结果。

guideCell.setupCode:
import numpy as np
name = np.array(['小明','小天','小明','小红','小风','小明','小红','小风','小明','小天','小亮'])
# name 已预加载；它是一维 NumPy 字符串数组，每个元素是一位小朋友的姓名
guideCell.description: 题 1（基础）：已预加载变量 name，它是一维 NumPy 字符串数组，每个元素是一位小朋友姓名。请用 np.unique(name, return_inverse=True)、np.zeros 和 np.add.at 统计不同姓名出现次数，打印 小明次数=4, 不同姓名数=5。

passRule.expectedSubstring: 小明次数=4, 不同姓名数=5

guideCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：小明次数=4, 不同姓名数=5。
guideCell.codeBackdropLabel: 提示：请按题意补全 NumPy 代码，运行后观察终端输出。
guideCell.codeBackdropCode:
unique, inverse = np.unique(name, return_inverse=True)
count = np.zeros(len(unique), dtype=int)
np.add.at(count, inverse, 1)
result = dict(zip(unique, count))
print(f"小明次数={result['小明']}, 不同姓名数={len(unique)}")

extensionCell.promptHtml: 题 2（扩展）：已预加载变量 name，它是一维 NumPy 字符串数组。请按照课件规则构造姓名相似度矩阵：自己和自己为 1；不同位置但姓名相同，则值为该姓名在除自己外全部同学中的占比；姓名不同则为 0。请打印矩阵形状、第 0 位和第 2 位同名相似度，格式为 矩阵形状=(11, 11), 小明同名相似度=0.30。
extensionCell.starterCode: # name 已预加载，请在下方构造 sim_matrix
extensionCell.setupCode:
import numpy as np
name = np.array(['小明','小天','小明','小红','小风','小明','小红','小风','小明','小天','小亮'])
# name 已预加载；它是一维 NumPy 字符串数组，每个元素是一位小朋友的姓名

passRule.expectedSubstring: 矩阵形状=(11, 11), 小明同名相似度=0.30

extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：矩阵形状=(11, 11), 小明同名相似度=0.30。

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
本章知识点的扩展练习

综合扩展题把数组创建、属性、切片、运算、比较和实践案例放在一起练习。本块包含 2 道题。

  • 综合扩展 1：学生成绩矩阵统计。
  • 综合扩展 2：成绩标准化与条件筛选。

预加载资料字段说明：本块题目会预加载变量 scores，它是二维 NumPy 数组，每行是一名学生，每列是一门课程成绩。

guideCell.setupCode:
import numpy as np

guideCell.description: 综合扩展 3：创建矩阵 A=[[1,2],[3,4]] 和 B=[[2,0],[1,2]]，用矩阵乘法 @ 得到 C，打印 C 的形状和右下角元素，格式为 乘积形状=(2, 2), 右下角=8。

extensionCell.starterCode: # name 已预加载，请统计并排序
extensionCell.setupCode:
import numpy as np
name = np.array(['小明','小天','小明','小红','小风','小明','小红','小风','小明','小天','小亮'])

passRule.expectedSubstring: 最高频姓名=小明, 次数=4

extensionCell.exerciseTitle: 综合扩展-4 姓名统计排序
extensionCell.expectedOutput: 屏幕显示：最高频姓名=小明, 次数=4。
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
unique, inverse = np.unique(name, return_inverse=True)

print(f"最高频姓名={pairs[0][0]}, 次数={int(pairs[0][1])}")

published_content.chapterIntroHtml:
第七章 Python 数据可视化

本章围绕 pandas 与 matplotlib 展开，统一使用课件目录中的 Kobe Bryant Shot Selection.csv、obama_address.txt 与 trump_address.txt 设计练习。为适配学生端浏览器运行环境，题目会把原始数据隐藏预加载为 df、OBAMA_TEXT、TRUMP_TEXT；学生编辑框只需要填写查询、统计或绘图代码。

  • Kobe CSV 练习覆盖 Series、DataFrame、read_csv、条件查询、缺失值、groupby、value_counts、cut 和 agg。
  • 绘图练习的数据也已预加载，学生只需完成筛选、聚合和 matplotlib 绘图语句，并在末尾打印指定中文标记用于判分。
  • 文本可视化练习使用 Obama 与 Trump 演讲文本，先做词频统计，为后续词云图理解打基础。

预加载资料字段说明：本章 Kobe 投篮数据已预加载为 df，它是一张 pandas DataFrame。可用字段包括：shot_id（投篮编号）、game_date（比赛日期）、period（第几节）、minutes_remaining（本节剩余分钟）、seconds_remaining（本节剩余秒）、shot_made_flag（是否命中，1 表示命中，0 表示未命中，空值表示缺失）、combined_shot_type（投篮类型）、shot_zone_range（投篮区域/距离范围）、shot_distance（投篮距离）、loc_x（横坐标）、loc_y（纵坐标）、season（赛季）、game_id（比赛编号）、opponent（对手）。写代码时用 df['字段名'] 取列，例如 df['shot_distance']。

文本资料变量说明：Obama 演讲文本已预加载为 OBAMA_TEXT，Trump 演讲文本已预加载为 TRUMP_TEXT。二者都是字符串，可用 .lower()、.split() 或 re.findall(...) 做英文单词统计。

词云图练习：学生端已预装 wordcloud 包，可使用 from wordcloud import WordCloud 生成词云对象，再用 plt.imshow(...) 和 plt.show() 显示在图形输出区域。

7.5 基于 pandas 的 CSV 文件读取

示例说明：下面这段示例展示题目怎样把指定的 Kobe CSV 样本读成 df。这部分由系统隐藏预加载，学生练习时直接基于 df 查询。

from io import StringIO
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(KOBE_CSV))

blocks.sectionTitle: 7.6 条件查询、缺失值与统计计算
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
7.6 条件查询、缺失值与统计计算

条件查询会返回布尔序列，再用它筛选行。多个条件组合时使用 &、|、~。Kobe 表的 shot_made_flag 中包含缺失值，适合练习缺失值过滤和均值统计。

示例说明：下面这段示例先过滤缺失命中记录，再筛选命中投篮，目的是演示布尔条件怎样一步步缩小 DataFrame 范围。

clean = df[~df.shot_made_flag.isna()]
made = clean[clean.shot_made_flag == 1.0]

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：筛选最后时刻命中记录。
  • 题 2：过滤缺失值并计算平均命中率。

guideCell.description:

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
7.7 分组、聚合与区间划分

groupby 可以按赛季、对手或投篮区域分组，再统计数量、均值、最大值等。value_counts() 常用于类别频数统计，pd.cut() 可以把投篮距离划分成区间。

示例说明：下面这段示例展示两类常用统计：groupby 计算每个赛季命中率，value_counts 统计不同投篮类型出现次数。

df.groupby('season')['shot_made_flag'].mean()
df['combined_shot_type'].value_counts()

本节练习 3 题：

  • 题 1：按赛季计算平均命中率。
  • 题 2：统计投篮类型次数。

blocks.sectionTitle: 7.7 续：cut 区间划分与 agg 聚合
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
7.7 续：cut 区间划分与 agg 聚合

投篮距离 shot_distance 是连续数值，可用 pd.cut 划分为篮下、中距离、远距离等区间。agg 可以在一次分组中同时计算多种统计量。

  • 题 3：把投篮距离划分为 4 个区间。
  • 小自测：按对手聚合出手次数和命中率。

guideCell.description:
题 3：过滤缺失命中记录后，把 shot_distance 按 [-1, 8, 16, 24, 100] 分箱，打印 分箱样本=109。

blocks.sectionTitle: 7.8 matplotlib 绘图环境与折线图
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
7.8 matplotlib 绘图环境与折线图

matplotlib.pyplot 常写作 plt。本节使用 Kobe 表中按赛季聚合出的命中率画折线图，原始数据由题目预加载，学生只需要完成聚合和绘图语句。

示例说明：下面这段示例把按赛季聚合后的命中率画成折线图，横轴代表赛季顺序，纵轴代表平均命中率，用来观察趋势。

plot_data = clean.groupby('season')['shot_made_flag'].mean()
plt.plot(range(len(plot_data)), plot_data)

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：绘制赛季命中率折线图。
  • 题 2：旋转 x 轴标签并补充标题。

预加载资料字段说明：本章 Kobe 投篮数据已预加载为 df，它是一张 pandas DataFrame。可用字段包括：shot_id（投篮编号）、game_date（比赛日期）、period（第几节）、minutes_remaining（本节剩余分钟）、seconds_remaining（本节剩余秒）、shot_made_flag（是否命中，1 表示命中，0 表示未命中，空值表示缺失）、combined_shot_type（投篮类型）、shot_zone_range（投篮区域/距离范围）、shot_distance（投篮距离）、loc_x（横坐标）、loc_y（纵坐标）、season（赛季）、game_id（比赛编号）、opponent（对手）。写代码时用 df['字段名'] 取列，例如 df['shot_distance']。

clean = df.dropna(subset=['shot_made_flag'])

blocks.knowledgeHtml:
7.9 饼图、散点图与直方图

饼图适合展示类别占比，散点图适合观察投篮位置分布，直方图适合观察投篮距离分布。本节绘图都使用预加载的 Kobe 表，学生只完成查询和绘图。

示例说明：下面三行分别对应三类图：pie 展示类别占比，scatter 展示二维位置分布，hist 展示数值分布。后面的练习会把 Kobe 表查询结果传给这些函数。

plt.pie(plot_data, labels=plot_data.index)
plt.scatter(x, y)
plt.hist(values)

本节练习 3 题：

  • 题 1：命中投篮区域饼图。
  • 题 2：指定比赛日期的投篮位置散点图。
  • 题 3：命中投篮距离直方图。

blocks.sectionTitle: 7.10 箱线图、保存图像与文本可视化思路
blocks.knowledgeHtml:
7.10 箱线图、保存图像与文本可视化思路

箱线图适合比较不同组的分布差异。savefig 可以把图保存为图片。文本可视化的第一步是词频统计，本节文本练习统一使用课件目录中的 obama_address.txt 和 trump_address.txt，题目会隐藏预加载文本内容。

示例说明：下面这段示例展示箱线图与保存图片的基本写法，箱线图用于比较不同赛季的分布，savefig 用于把图保存成文件。

plt.boxplot(data, labels=labels)
plt.savefig('chart.png')

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：按赛季绘制命中率箱线图。
  • 题 2：统计 Obama 与 Trump 演讲文本词频。

文本资料变量说明：Obama 演讲文本已预加载为 OBAMA_TEXT，Trump 演讲文本已预加载为 TRUMP_TEXT。二者都是字符串，可用 .lower()、.split() 或 re.findall(...) 做英文单词统计。

guideCell.description:
题 1（基础）：过滤缺失命中记录，按赛季收集每场比赛命中率，绘制箱线图并保存为 fg_box.png，最后打印 箱线图赛季数=7。

预加载资料字段说明：本章 Kobe 投篮数据已预加载为 df，它是一张 pandas DataFrame。可用字段包括：shot_id（投篮编号）、game_date（比赛日期）、period（第几节）、minutes_remaining（本节剩余分钟）、seconds_remaining（本节剩余秒）、shot_made_flag（是否命中，1 表示命中，0 表示未命中，空值表示缺失）、combined_shot_type（投篮类型）、shot_zone_range（投篮区域/距离范围）、shot_distance（投篮距离）、loc_x（横坐标）、loc_y（纵坐标）、season（赛季）、game_id（比赛编号）、opponent（对手）。写代码时用 df['字段名'] 取列，例如 df['shot_distance']。
guideCell.exerciseTitle: 7.10-题1 赛季命中率箱线图与 savefig
guideCell.expectedOutput: 图形区出现箱线图；终端显示：箱线图赛季数=7
guideCell.referenceAnswer:
clean = df.dropna(subset=['shot_made_flag'])
plot_series = clean.groupby(['season', 'game_id'])['shot_made_flag'].mean()
seasons = sorted(clean.season.unique())
data = [list(plot_series[(season,)]) for season in seasons]
plt.boxplot(data, labels=seasons)
plt.savefig('fg_box.png')
plt.show()
print(f"箱线图赛季数={len(seasons)}")

extensionCell.starterCode: # 数据已预加载，请在下方补全查询、统计或绘图代码
extensionCell.setupCode:
import re
from collections import Counter
import pandas as pd
OBAMA_TEXT = 'Obama Inaugural Address
20th January 2009

My fellow citizens:

I stand here today humbled by the task before us, grateful for the trust you have bestowed, mindful of the sacrifices borne by our ancestors. I thank President Bush for his service to our nation, as well as the generosity and cooperation he has shown throughout this transition.

Forty-four Americans have now taken the presidential oath. The words have been spoken during rising tides of prosperity and the still waters of peace. Yet, every so often the oath is taken amidst gathering clouds and raging storms. At these moments, America has carried on not simply because of the skill or vision of those in high office, but because We the People have remained faithful to the ideals of our forbearers, and true to our founding documents.

So it has been. So it must be with this generation of Americans.

That we are in the midst of crisis is now well understood. Our nation is at war, against a far-reaching network of violence and hatred. Our economy is badly weakened, a consequence of greed and irresponsibility on the part of some, but also our collective failure to make hard choices and prepare the nation for a new age. Homes have been lost; jobs shed; businesses shuttered. Our health care is too costly; our schools fail too many; and each day brings further evidence that the ways we use energy strengthen our adversaries and threaten our planet.

These are the indicators of crisis, subject to data and statistics. Less measurable but no less profound is a sapping of confidence across our land - a nagging fear that America's decline is inevitable, and that the next generation must lower its sights.

Today I say to you that the challenges we face are real. They are serious and they are many. They will not be met easily or in a short span of time. But know this, America - they will be met.

On this day, we gather because we have chosen hope over fear, unity of purpose over conflict and discord.

On this day, we come to proclaim an end to the petty grievances and false promises, the recriminations and worn out dogmas, that for far too long have strangled our politics.

We remain a young nation, but in the words of Scripture, the time has come to set aside childish things. The time has come to reaffirm our enduring spirit; to choose our better history; to carry forward that precious gift, that noble idea, passed on from generation to generation: the God-given promise that all are equal, all are free, and all deserve a chance to pursue their full measure of happiness.

In reaffirming the greatness of our nation, we understand that greatness is never a given. It must be earned. Our journey has never been one of short-cuts or settling for less. It has not been the path for the faint-hearted - for those who prefer leisure over work, or seek only the pleasures of riches and fame. Rather, it has been the risk-takers, the doers, the makers of things - some celebrated but more often men and women obscure in their labor, who have carried us up the long, rugged path towards prosperity and freedom.

For us, they packed up their few worldly possessions and traveled across oceans in search of a new life.

For us, they toiled in sweatshops and settled the West; endured the lash of the whip and plowed the hard earth.

For us, they fought and died, in places like Concord and Gettysburg; Normandy and Khe Sahn.

Time and again these men and women struggled and sacrificed and worked till their hands were raw so that we might live a better life. They saw America as bigger than the sum of our individual ambitions; greater than all the differences of birth or wealth or faction.

This is the journey we continue today. We remain the most prosperous, powerful nation on Earth. Our workers are no less productive than when this crisis began. Our minds are no less inventive, our goods and services no less needed than they were last week or last month or last year. Our capacity remains undiminished. But our time of standing pat, of protecting narrow interests and putting off unpleasant decisions - that time has surely passed. Starting today, we must pick ourselves up, dust ourselves off, and begin again the work of remaking America.

For everywhere we look, there is work to be done. The state of the economy calls for action, bold and swift, and we will act - not only to create new jobs, but to lay a new foundation for growth. We will build the roads and bridges, the electric grids and digital lines that feed our commerce and bind us together. We will restore science to its rightful place, and wield technology's wonders to raise health care's quality and lower its cost. We will harness the sun and the winds and the soil to fuel our cars and run our factories. And we will transform our schools and colleges and universities to meet the demands of a new age. All this we can do. And all this we will do.

Now, there are some who question the scale of our ambitions - who suggest that our system cannot tolerate too many big plans. Their memories are short. For they have forgotten what this country has already done; what free men and women can achieve when imagination is joined to common purpose, and necessity to courage.

What the cynics fail to understand is that the ground has shifted beneath them - that the stale political arguments that have consumed us for so long no longer apply. The question we ask today is not whether our government is too big or too small, but whether it works - whether it helps families find jobs at a decent wage, care they can afford, a retirement that is dignified. Where the answer is yes, we intend to move forward. Where the answer is no, programs will end. And those of us who manage the public's dollars will be held to account - to spend wisely, reform bad habits, and do our business in the light of day - because only then can we restore the vital trust between a people and their government.
Nor is the question before us whether the market is a force for good or ill. Its power to generate wealth and expand freedom is unmatched, but this crisis has reminded us that without a watchful eye, the market can spin out of control - and that a nation cannot prosper long when it favors only the prosperous. The success of our economy has always depended not just on the size of our Gross Domestic Product, but on the reach of our prosperity; on the ability to extend opportunity to every willing heart - not out of charity, but because it is the surest route to our common good.

As for our common defense, we reject as false the choice between our safety and our ideals. Our Founding Fathers, faced with perils we can scarcely imagine, drafted a charter to assure the rule of law and the rights of man, a charter expanded by the blood of generations. Those ideals still light the world, and we will not give them up for expedience's sake. And so to all other peoples and governments who are watching today, from the grandest capitals to the small village where my father was born: know that America is a friend of each nation and every man, woman, and child who seeks a future of peace and dignity, and we are ready to lead once more.

Recall that earlier generations faced down fascism and communism not just with missiles and tanks, but with the sturdy alliances and enduring convictions. They understood that our power alone cannot protect us, nor does it entitle us to do as we please. Instead, they knew that our power grows through its prudent use; our security emanates from the justness of our cause, the force of our example, the tempering qualities of humility and restraint.

We will begin to responsibly leave Iraq to its people, and forge a hard-earned peace in Afghanistan. With old friends and former foes, we’ll work tirelessly to lessen the nuclear threat, and roll back the specter of a warming planet. We will not apologize for our way of life, nor will we waver in its defense, and for those who seek to advance their aims by inducing terror and slaughtering innocents, we say to you now that our spirit is stronger and cannot be broken; you cannot outlast us, and we will defeat you.

For we know that our patchwork heritage is a strength, not a weakness. We are a nation of Christians and Muslims, Jews and Hindus - and non-believers. We are shaped by every language and culture, drawn from every end of this Earth; and because we have tasted the bitter swill of civil war and segregation, and emerged from that dark chapter stronger and more united, we cannot help but believe that the old hatreds shall someday pass; that the lines of tribe shall soon dissolve; that as the world grows smaller, our common humanity shall reveal itself; and that America must play its role in ushering in a new era of peace.

To those leaders around the globe who seek to sow conflict, or blame their society's ills on the West - know that your people will judge you on what you can build, not what you destroy. To those who cling to power through corruption and deceit and the silencing of dissent, know that you are on the wrong side of history; but that we will extend a hand if you are willing to unclench your fist.

To the people of poor nations, we pledge to work alongside you to make your farms flourish and let clean waters flow; to nourish starved bodies and feed hungry minds. And to those nations like ours that enjoy relative plenty, we say we can no longer afford indifference to the suffering outside our borders; nor can we consume the world's resources without regard to effect. For the world has changed, and we must change with it.

They have something to tell us, just as the fallen heroes who lie in Arlington whisper through the ages. We honor them not only because they are guardians of our liberty, but because they embody the spirit of service; a willingness to find meaning in something greater than themselves. And yet, at this moment - a moment that will define a generation - it is precisely this spirit that must inhabit us all.

For as much as government can do and must do, it is ultimately the faith and determination of the American people upon which this nation relies. It is the kindness to take in a stranger when the levees break, the selflessness of workers who would rather cut their hours than see a friend lose their job which sees us through our darkest hours. It is the firefighter's courage to storm a stairway filled with smoke, but also a parent's willingness to nurture a child, that finally decides our fate.

Our challenges may be new. The instruments with which we meet them may be new. But those values upon which our success depends - honesty and hard work, courage and fair play, tolerance and curiosity, loyalty and patriotism - these things are old. These things are true. They have been the quiet force of progress throughout our history. What is demanded then is a return to these truths. What is required of us now is a new era of responsibility - a recognition, on the part of every American, that we have duties to ourselves, our nation, and the world, duties that we do not grudgingly accept but rather seize gladly, firm in the knowledge that there is nothing so satisfying to the spirit, so defining of our character, than giving our all to a difficult task.

This is the price and the promise of citizenship.

This is the source of our confidence - the knowledge that God calls on us to shape an uncertain destiny.

This is the meaning of our liberty and our creed - why men and women and children of every race and every faith can join in celebration across this magnificent mall, and why a man whose father less than sixty years ago might not have been served at a local restaurant can now stand before you to take a most sacred oath.

So let us mark this day with remembrance, of who we are and how far we have traveled. In the year of America's birth, in the coldest of months, a small band of patriots huddled by dying campfires on the shores of an icy river. The capital was abandoned. The enemy was advancing. The snow was stained with blood. At a moment when the outcome of our revolution was most in doubt, the father of our nation ordered these words be read to the people:
"Let it be told to the future world...that in the depth of winter, when nothing but hope and virtue could survive...that the city and the country, alarmed at one common danger, came forth to meet [it]."

America. In the face of our common dangers, in this winter of our hardship, let us remember these timeless words. With hope and virtue, let us brave once more the icy currents, and endure what storms may come. Let it be said by our children's children that when we were tested we refused to let this journey end, that we did not turn back nor did we falter; and with eyes fixed on the horizon and God's grace upon us, we carried forth that great gift of freedom and delivered it safely to future generations.

Thank you. God bless you and God bless the United States of America.'
TRUMP_TEXT = 'Chief Justice Roberts, President Carter, President Clinton, President Bush, President Obama, fellow Americans, and people of the world, thank you. Today’s ceremony, however, has very special meaning, because today we are not merely transferring power from one administration to another, or from one party to another, but we are transferring power from Washington, D.C., and giving it back to you, the people.

For too long, a small group in our nation’s capital has reaped the rewards of government, while the people have borne the cost. Washington flourished, but the people did not share in its wealth. Their triumphs have not been your triumphs, and while they celebrated in our nation’s capital, there was little to celebrate for struggling families all across our land. That all changes, starting right here and right now, because this moment is your moment --- it belongs to you. It belongs to everyone gathered here today, and everyone watching, all across America. This is your day. This is your celebration, and this, the United States of America, is your country.


What truly matters is not which party controls our government, but whether our government is controlled by the people. January 20th, 2017 will be remembered as the day the people became the rulers of this nation again. The forgotten men and women of our country, will be forgotten no longer. Everyone is listening to you now. You came by the tens of millions to become part of a historic movement, the likes of which the world has never seen before. At the center of this movement is a crucial conviction, that a nation exists to serve its citizens. Americans want great schools for their children, safe neighborhoods for their families, and good jobs for themselves. These are just and reasonable demands of righteous people and a righteous public, but for too many of our citizens a different reality exists. Mothers and children trapped in poverty in our inner cities, rusted out factories, scattered like tombstones across the across the landscape of our nation, an education system flush with cash, but which leaves our young and beautiful students deprived of all knowledge, and the crime, and the gangs, and the drugs that have stolen too many lives and robbed our country of so much unrealized potential. This American carnage stops right here and stops right now.

We are one nation and their pain is our pain. Their dreams are our dreams and their success will be our success. We share one heart, one home, and one glorious destiny. The oath of office, I take today, is an oath of allegiance to all Americans. For many decades, we’ve enriched foreign industry at the expense of American industry, subsidized the armies of other countries, while allowing for the very sad depletion of our military. We’ve defended other nation’s borders while refusing to defend our own. And spent trillions and trillions of dollars overseas, while America’s infrastructure has fallen into disrepair and decay. We’ve made other countries rich while the wealth, strength and confidence of our country has dissipated over the horizon. One by one, the factories shuddered and left our shores, with not even a thought about the millions and millions of American workers that were left behind. We will bring back our borders. We will bring back our wealth, and we will bring back our dreams. blocks.knowledgeHtml:
7.11 wordcloud 词云图

wordcloud 可以把文本中的高频词转成词云图。本节继续使用已预加载的 OBAMA_TEXT 和 TRUMP_TEXT，先统计高频词，再把两段演讲文本分别生成词云。

示例说明：先导入 WordCloud，用 generate(text) 生成词云对象，再用 plt.imshow(wordcloud, interpolation='bilinear') 显示，最后调用 plt.show()。

from wordcloud import WordCloud as WC
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
cloud = WC(background_color='white').generate(OBAMA_TEXT)
plt.imshow(cloud, interpolation='bilinear')
plt.axis('off')
plt.show()

本节练习 2 题：

  • 题 1：统计 Obama 文本中出现次数最多的英文单词。
  • 题 2：生成 Obama/Trump 双词云图。

文本资料变量说明：Obama 演讲文本已预加载为 OBAMA_TEXT，Trump 演讲文本已预加载为 TRUMP_TEXT。二者都是字符串，可用 .lower()、.split() 或 re.findall(...) 做英文单词统计。

Homes have been lost; jobs shed; businesses shuttered. On this day, we come to proclaim an end to the petty grievances and false promises, the recriminations and worn out dogmas, that for far too long have strangled our politics.

We must speak our minds openly, debate our disagreements, but always pursue solidarity. When America is united, America is totally unstoppable. There should be no fear. We are protected, and we will always be protected. We will be protected by the great men and women of our military and law enforcement. And most importantly, we will be protected by God.

Finally, we must think big and dream even bigger. In America, we understand that a nation is only living as long as it is striving. We will no longer accept politicians who are all talk and no action, constantly complaining but never doing anything about it. The time for empty talk is over. Now arrives the hour of action. Do not allow anyone to tell you that it cannot be done. No challenge can match the heart and fight and spirit of America. We will not fail. Our country will thrive and prosper again.

We stand at the birth of a new millennium, ready to unlock the mysteries of space, to free the Earth from the miseries of disease and to harness the industries and technologies of tomorrow. A new national pride will stir our souls, lift our sights and heal our divisions. It’s time to remember that old wisdom our soldiers will never forget, that whether we are black, or brown, or white, we all bleed the same red blood of patriots. We all enjoy the same glorious freedoms, and we all salute the same, great American flag. And whether a child is born in the urban sprawl of Detroit or the windswept plains of Nebraska, they look up at the at the same night sky, they fill their heart with the same dreams and they are infused with the breath of life by the same almighty creator.

So to all Americans, in every city near and far, small and large, from mountain to mountain, from ocean to ocean, hear these words. You will never be ignored again. Your voice, your hopes, and your dreams will define our American destiny. And your courage and goodness and love, will forever guide us along the way. Together, we will make America strong again. We will make America wealthy again. We will make America proud again We will make America safe again, And yes, together, we will make we will make America great again. Thank you. God bless you. And god bless America. Thank you. God bless America.'

extensionCell.setupCode:
import re
from collections import Counter
import pandas as pd
OBAMA_TEXT = 'Obama Inaugural Address
20th January 2009

My fellow citizens:

I stand here today humbled by the task before us, grateful for the trust you have bestowed, mindful of the sacrifices borne by our ancestors. I thank President Bush for his service to our nation, as well as the generosity and cooperation he has shown throughout this transition.

Forty-four Americans have now taken the presidential oath. The words have been spoken during rising tides of prosperity and the still waters of peace. Yet, every so often the oath is taken amidst gathering clouds and raging storms. At these moments, America has carried on not simply because of the skill or vision of those in high office, but because We the People have remained faithful to the ideals of our forbearers, and true to our founding documents.

So it has been. So it must be with this generation of Americans.

That we are in the midst of crisis is now well understood. Our nation is at war, against a far-reaching network of violence and hatred. Our economy is badly weakened, a consequence of greed and irresponsibility on the part of some, but also our collective failure to make hard choices and prepare the nation for a new age. Homes have been lost; jobs shed; businesses shuttered. Our health care is too costly; our schools fail too many; and each day brings further evidence that the ways we use energy strengthen our adversaries and threaten our planet.

These are the indicators of crisis, subject to data and statistics. Less measurable but no less profound is a sapping of confidence across our land - a nagging fear that America's decline is inevitable, and that the next generation must lower its sights.

Today I say to you that the challenges we face are real. They are serious and they are many. They will not be met easily or in a short span of time. But know this, America - they will be met.

On this day, we gather because we have chosen hope over fear, unity of purpose over conflict and discord.

On this day, we come to proclaim an end to the petty grievances and false promises, the recriminations and worn out dogmas, that for far too long have strangled our politics.

We remain a young nation, but in the words of Scripture, the time has come to set aside childish things. The time has come to reaffirm our enduring spirit; to choose our better history; to carry forward that precious gift, that noble idea, passed on from generation to generation: the God-given promise that all are equal, all are free, and all deserve a chance to pursue their full measure of happiness.

In reaffirming the greatness of our nation, we understand that greatness is never a given. It must be earned. Our journey has never been one of short-cuts or settling for less. It has not been the path for the faint-hearted - for those who prefer leisure over work, or seek only the pleasures of riches and fame. Rather, it has been the risk-takers, the doers, the makers of things - some celebrated but more often men and women obscure in their labor, who have carried us up the long, rugged path towards prosperity and freedom.

For us, they packed up their few worldly possessions and traveled across oceans in search of a new life.

For us, they toiled in sweatshops and settled the West; endured the lash of the whip and plowed the hard earth.

For us, they fought and died, in places like Concord and Gettysburg; Normandy and Khe Sahn.

Time and again these men and women struggled and sacrificed and worked till their hands were raw so that we might live a better life. They saw America as bigger than the sum of our individual ambitions; greater than all the differences of birth or wealth or faction.

This is the journey we continue today. We remain the most prosperous, powerful nation on Earth. Our workers are no less productive than when this crisis began. Our minds are no less inventive, our goods and services no less needed than they were last week or last month or last year. Our capacity remains undiminished. But our time of standing pat, of protecting narrow interests and putting off unpleasant decisions - that time has surely passed. Starting today, we must pick ourselves up, dust ourselves off, and begin again the work of remaking America.

For everywhere we look, there is work to be done. The state of the economy calls for action, bold and swift, and we will act - not only to create new jobs, but to lay a new foundation for growth. We will build the roads and bridges, the electric grids and digital lines that feed our commerce and bind us together. We will restore science to its rightful place, and wield technology's wonders to raise health care's quality and lower its cost. We will harness the sun and the winds and the soil to fuel our cars and run our factories. And we will transform our schools and colleges and universities to meet the demands of a new age. All this we can do. And all this we will do.

Now, there are some who question the scale of our ambitions - who suggest that our system cannot tolerate too many big plans. Their memories are short. For they have forgotten what this country has already done; what free men and women can achieve when imagination is joined to common purpose, and necessity to courage.

What the cynics fail to understand is that the ground has shifted beneath them - that the stale political arguments that have consumed us for so long no longer apply. The question we ask today is not whether our government is too big or too small, but whether it works - whether it helps families find jobs at a decent wage, care they can afford, a retirement that is dignified. Where the answer is yes, we intend to move forward. Where the answer is no, programs will end. And those of us who manage the public's dollars will be held to account - to spend wisely, reform bad habits, and do our business in the light of day - because only then can we restore the vital trust between a people and their government.
Nor is the question before us whether the market is a force for good or ill. Its power to generate wealth and expand freedom is unmatched, but this crisis has reminded us that without a watchful eye, the market can spin out of control - and that a nation cannot prosper long when it favors only the prosperous. The success of our economy has always depended not just on the size of our Gross Domestic Product, but on the reach of our prosperity; on the ability to extend opportunity to every willing heart - not out of charity, but because it is the surest route to our common good.

As for our common defense, we reject as false the choice between our safety and our ideals. Our Founding Fathers, faced with perils we can scarcely imagine, drafted a charter to assure the rule of law and the rights of man, a charter expanded by the blood of generations. Those ideals still light the world, and we will not give them up for expedience's sake. And so to all other peoples and governments who are watching today, from the grandest capitals to the small village where my father was born: know that America is a friend of each nation and every man, woman, and child who seeks a future of peace and dignity, and we are ready to lead once more.

Recall that earlier generations faced down fascism and communism not just with missiles and tanks, but with the sturdy alliances and enduring convictions. They understood that our power alone cannot protect us, nor does it entitle us to do as we please. Instead, they knew that our power grows through its prudent use; our security emanates from the justness of our cause, the force of our example, the tempering qualities of humility and restraint.

We are the keepers of this legacy. Guided by these principles once more, we can meet those new threats that demand even greater effort - even greater cooperation and understanding between nations. We will begin to responsibly leave Iraq to its people, and forge a hard-earned peace in Afghanistan. With old friends and former foes, we’ll work tirelessly to lessen the nuclear threat, and roll back the specter of a warming planet. We will not apologize for our way of life, nor will we waver in its defense, and for those who seek to advance their aims by inducing terror and slaughtering innocents, we say to you now that our spirit is stronger and cannot be broken; you cannot outlast us, and we will defeat you.

For we know that our patchwork heritage is a strength, not a weakness. We are a nation of Christians and Muslims, Jews and Hindus - and non-believers. We are shaped by every language and culture, drawn from every end of this Earth; and because we have tasted the bitter swill of civil war and segregation, and emerged from that dark chapter stronger and more united, we cannot help but believe that the old hatreds shall someday pass; that the lines of tribe shall soon dissolve; that as the world grows smaller, our common humanity shall reveal itself; and that America must play its role in ushering in a new era of peace.

To the Muslim world, we seek a new way forward, based on mutual interest and mutual respect. To those leaders around the globe who seek to sow conflict, or blame their society's ills on the West - know that your people will judge you on what you can build, not what you destroy. To those who cling to power through corruption and deceit and the silencing of dissent, know that you are on the wrong side of history; but that we will extend a hand if you are willing to unclench your fist.

To the people of poor nations, we pledge to work alongside you to make your farms flourish and let clean waters flow; to nourish starved bodies and feed hungry minds. And to those nations like ours that enjoy relative plenty, we say we can no longer afford indifference to the suffering outside our borders; nor can we consume the world's resources without regard to effect. For the world has changed, and we must change with it.

As we consider the road that unfolds before us, we remember with humble gratitude those brave Americans who, at this very hour, patrol far-off deserts and distant mountains. They have something to tell us, just as the fallen heroes who lie in Arlington whisper through the ages. We honor them not only because they are guardians of our liberty, but because they embody the spirit of service; a willingness to find meaning in something greater than themselves. And yet, at this moment - a moment that will define a generation - it is precisely this spirit that must inhabit us all.

For as much as government can do and must do, it is ultimately the faith and determination of the American people upon which this nation relies. It is the kindness to take in a stranger when the levees break, the selflessness of workers who would rather cut their hours than see a friend lose their job which sees us through our darkest hours. It is the firefighter's courage to storm a stairway filled with smoke, but also a parent's willingness to nurture a child, that finally decides our fate.

Our challenges may be new. The instruments with which we meet them may be new. But those values upon which our success depends - honesty and hard work, courage and fair play, tolerance and curiosity, loyalty and patriotism - these things are old. These things are true. They have been the quiet force of progress throughout our history. What is demanded then is a return to these truths. What is required of us now is a new era of responsibility - a recognition, on the part of every American, that we have duties to ourselves, our nation, and the world, duties that we do not grudgingly accept but rather seize gladly, firm in the knowledge that there is nothing so satisfying to the spirit, so defining of our character, than giving our all to a difficult task.

This is the price and the promise of citizenship.

This is the source of our confidence - the knowledge that God calls on us to shape an uncertain destiny.

This is the meaning of our liberty and our creed - why men and women and children of every race and every faith can join in celebration across this magnificent mall, and why a man whose father less than sixty years ago might not have been served at a local restaurant can now stand before you to take a most sacred oath.

So let us mark this day with remembrance, of who we are and how far we have traveled. In the year of America's birth, in the coldest of months, a small band of patriots huddled by dying campfires on the shores of an icy river. The capital was abandoned. The enemy was advancing. The snow was stained with blood. At a moment when the outcome of our revolution was most in doubt, the father of our nation ordered these words be read to the people:
"Let it be told to the future world...that in the depth of winter, when nothing but hope and virtue could survive...that the city and the country, alarmed at one common danger, came forth to meet [it]."

America. In the face of our common dangers, in this winter of our hardship, let us remember these timeless words. With hope and virtue, let us brave once more the icy currents, and endure what storms may come. Let it be said by our children's children that when we were tested we refused to let this journey end, that we did not turn back nor did we falter; and with eyes fixed on the horizon and God's grace upon us, we carried forth that great gift of freedom and delivered it safely to future generations.

Thank you. God bless you and God bless the United States of America.'

extensionCell.expectedImageAlt: 参考图：左侧为 Obama 演讲词云，右侧为 Trump 演讲词云。
extensionCell.referenceAnswer:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from wordcloud import WordCloud as WC

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
for idx, (title, text) in enumerate([('Obama', OBAMA_TEXT), ('Trump', TRUMP_TEXT)], start=1):
    wordcloud = WC(background_color='white', max_words=100, width=800, height=400, random_state=7).generate(text)
    plt.subplot(1, 2, idx)
    plt.imshow(wordcloud, interpolation='bilinear')
    plt.title(f'{title} WordCloud')
    plt.axis('off')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print('词云图完成')
